UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific settings, preferences, and profile information for the SalesStream CRM application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID). (Primary Key)

profilePicURL.description: URL to the user's profile picture stored in Firebase Storage.

status.description: The user's current status (e.g., 'Available', 'Busy', 'In a meeting', 'Do not disturb', or a custom message).

statusUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user's status was last updated.

timeZone.description: The user's selected timezone, typically an IANA timezone identifier (e.g., 'America/New_York').

notificationSoundsEnabled.description: Indicates if notification sounds are enabled for the user.

defaultLeadStatus.description: The default status assigned to new leads created by this user.

compactModeEnabled.description: Indicates if compact mode (smaller fonts/tables) is enabled for the UI.

autoArchiveClosedLeadsAfterDays.description: Number of days after which closed leads (won/lost) are automatically archived. A value of 0 or null might mean no auto-archiving.

language.description: The preferred language for the application UI (e.g., 'en', 'es').

currency.description: The preferred currency for deal values (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

tabLayouts.description: Stores custom layout preferences for different application tabs/sections, e.g., column visibility and order.

Lead.title: Lead
Lead.description: Represents a potential sales opportunity or contact within the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lead. (Primary Key)

ownerUid.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who owns this lead. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Lead)

name.description: Full name of the lead or company name.

company.description: Company name associated with the lead.

status.description: Current sales pipeline status of the lead (e.g., 'new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'proposal', 'won', 'lost').

tagIds.description: References to Tag entities associated with this lead. (Relationship: Lead N:N Tag)

leadScore.description: A numerical score indicating the potential value or likelihood of closing the lead.

nextFollowUpAt.description: The scheduled date and time for the next follow-up activity.

lastContactAt.description: The date and time of the last recorded contact with the lead.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lead was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the lead was last updated.

customFields.description: A flexible dictionary for storing additional, user-defined fields for the lead.

notesCount.description: Denormalized count of notes associated with this lead.

callsCount.description: Denormalized count of calls associated with this lead.

Activity.title: Activity
Activity.description: Records an interaction or event related to a specific lead.

id.description: Unique identifier for the activity. (Primary Key)

leadId.description: Reference to the Lead entity this activity belongs to. (Relationship: Lead 1:N Activity)

content.description: Detailed description or notes about the activity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the activity was recorded.

oldStatus.description: (Optional) The lead's status before a status change activity.

newStatus.description: (Optional) The lead's status after a status change activity.

Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: Represents a reusable tag or category that can be applied to leads for organization and filtering.

id.description: Unique identifier for the tag. (Primary Key)

name.description: The name or label of the tag (e.g., 'Hot Lead', 'Enterprise', 'Follow-up').

ownerUid.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who created or owns this tag. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Tag)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tag was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tag was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user-specific settings, preferences, and profile information. The document ID `{userId}` corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID, making it directly accessible to the authenticated user. No denormalized authorization fields are explicitly needed beyond the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the owner of this UserProfile.

definition.description: Contains individual sales lead records for a specific user. The `{userId}` in the path indicates the owner. Includes denormalized 'ownerUid' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path and the document's `ownerUid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns this Lead.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Lead document.

definition.description: Records specific interactions or events related to a lead. Nested under a user's lead. Access relies on `request.auth.uid` matching `{userId}` and a security rule `get()` call to the parent Lead document to verify `Lead` ownership, as the `Activity` schema does not include a denormalized `ownerUid` field.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns the parent Lead.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Lead document.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Activity document.

definition.description: Stores reusable tags or categories created by and for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'ownerUid' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path and the document's `ownerUid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who owns this Tag.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Tag document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. It primarily utilizes a hierarchical, path-based ownership model, segregating all user-specific data under individual user documents. This approach simplifies security rules significantly by making authorization dependent almost entirely on the `request.auth.uid` matching a `userId` wildcard in the path.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:**

1.  **UserProfile (`/users/{userId}`):** The user's profile document (`UserProfile`) is directly accessible via their `uid`. Authorization is fully independent as `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}` in the path, ensuring a user can only access their own profile. No `get()` calls are needed in rules for this entity.

2.  **Lead (`/users/{userId}/leads/{leadId}`):** Each `Lead` document includes an `ownerUid` field, which stores the `userId` of the lead's owner. This field is denormalized directly into the `Lead` document. Security rules for `Lead` can then simply check `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path) and `resource.data.ownerUid == userId`, achieving full authorization independence without needing to query a parent document. This enables atomic creation and updates.

3.  **Activity (`/users/{userId}/leads/{leadId}/activities/{activityId}`):** The `Activity` entity's access logically depends on its parent `Lead`'s `ownerUid`. According to the Authorization Independence mandate, this `ownerUid` should be denormalized into the `Activity` document. *However, the provided `Activity` schema does not include an `ownerUid` field.* As per the strict adherence to the provided schema, `ownerUid` is omitted from the `Activity` document. Consequently, security rules for `Activity` will *require a `get()` call to the parent `Lead` document* to verify that the `Lead` (and thus its `Activities`) is owned by `request.auth.uid`. This constitutes a **violation** of the Authorization Independence principle for `Activity` data. For full compliance, the `Activity` schema should be updated to include `ownerUid` (denormalized from `Lead`).

4.  **Tag (`/users/{userId}/tags/{tagId}`):** Similar to `Lead`, each `Tag` document includes an `ownerUid` field, storing the `userId` of the tag's creator/owner. This denormalization makes `Tag` documents authorization independent, allowing rules to verify `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path) and `resource.data.ownerUid == userId` without `get()` calls.

**Querying for Authorization Purposes (QAPs - Rules are not Filters):**

The hierarchical structure ensures that `list` operations (queries) are inherently secure and efficient. When a user queries for their leads (`/users/{userId}/leads`), activities (`/users/{userId}/leads/{leadId}/activities`), or tags (`/users/{userId}/tags`), the `userId` in the path ensures that Firestore's security rules can immediately filter results to only include data owned by `request.auth.uid`. This prevents unauthorized data leakage and avoids costly or insecure client-side filtering. By placing all user-specific data under `/users/{userId}`, security rules can use a single, explicit check on the path wildcard, `request.auth.uid == userId`, combined with denormalized `ownerUid` fields for subcollections where applicable (Leads, Tags). This makes the intent clear and prevents rules from acting as data filters.
